summary: CUNY's six-year graduation rate for baccalaureate students has declined in each of the past three years, from 62.0% in Fiscal 2022 to 57.9% in Fiscal 2025. As with CUNY's other completion metrics, the decline coincides with a steep enrollment drop — community college enrollment fell to 66% of pre-pandemic levels — and City budget cuts of $155 million in Fiscal 2023 alone (235 lost positions), with a further $41 million in permanent annual cuts proposed for Fiscal 2024–2026 that the City Comptroller's office warns will further reduce course offerings and student support.

sources.label: Center for an Urban Future, "Closing NYC's College Attainment Gap"

oneLiner: decreasing since FY22, alongside a steep enrollment slide and City budget cuts to CUNY
